pages.full_text:
WHITE MOSS HOUSE

Rydal Water
Tel. Grasmere 35295

WEDNESDAY 3 MAY 1995

Carrot, coriander, lentil and orange soup
****
A bombe of brill with a centre of Bonchester Brie wrapped in smoked salmon
Sorrel and watercress sauce
****
Rack of Westmorland Herdwick spring lamb roasted with a herb crust of parsley, sage, rosemary and thyme (served pink unless otherwise requested)
Recurrant, mint and merlot sauce
Potato splits
Aubergines cooked with sesame oil and three peppers
Steamed cauliflower
Stir fried fine green beans
****
Sticky chocolate hazelnut cream slice
Cabinet pudding with fluffy lemon sauce (a hot, light steamed lemon pudding coated in raisins)
pages.notes: This page is a printed/typed restaurant menu card glued into the diary as a souvenir. The card is oriented sideways (rotated 90 degrees). 'Bonchester' refers to Bonchester Bridge / Bonchester Brie (a Scottish cheese). 'Recurrant' is the menu's spelling (recurrent/redcurrant). 'Westmorland Herdwick' — Westmorland is a historic county of the Lake District.

pages.full_text:
Sticky chocolate hazelnut cream slice
Cabinet pudding with fluffy lemon sauce (a hot, light steamed lemon pudding coated in raisins)
Banana ice cream
****
British cheeses with White Moss oat biscuits
***
Coffee

May we suggest a glass of Brown Brothers' Orange Muscat and Flora 19[illegible] with your dessert? £3.50

To avoid discomfort to others, guests are requested not to smoke in the dining room
pages.notes: Continuation of the White Moss House menu card from the left page. The right side also carries a decorative illustration of a country house, birds, and foliage. The vintage year after 'Brown Brothers' Orange Muscat and Flora' is obscured by the illustration — likely '199[x]'. 'White Moss oat biscuits' refers to the restaurant's own-made biscuits.
